headline: Space names crater on SpaceX IPO positioning; LatAm holds better
conviction_update: MELI down 1.5% to $1606 despite accumulating bullish coverage—multiple institutional endorsements this week position it as the LatAm compounder of choice over SE. The 7.3% post-earnings decline creating entry opportunity with 49% upside to PT; fintech credit growth thesis unchanged. NU off 1.9% to $13.76 on no idiosyncratic news, trading in sympathy with broader EM fintech weakness; 31% upside to $18 PT remains attractive at current multiples.

watchlist_movers.note: Down 6.8% as space complex reprices around imminent SpaceX IPO filing—satellite direct-to-cell thesis intact but expect volatility until SpaceX prices.
Matching 6.8% decline despite unrelated naval contract momentum elsewhere; SpaceX IPO narrative creating binary setup for independent launch providers.
UBS cuts PT to $60 (was ~$69); Microsoft-Nvidia nuclear push provides demand tailwind but execution timeline concerns persist at current valuation.

market_context: Risk-off session across frontier tech with space names bearing brunt of SpaceX IPO repositioning, while EM financials see modest outflows on broader dollar strength.
